Q2 2022 revenue=11.32B eps=0.44 vs Q1 2022 eps=0.64

CVX.rationale: P/E at 9.6x (near 1-year low) with 18.9% ROE and doubled YoY earnings offers attractive risk-adjusted reward, though oil-price-decline risk tempers conviction.
key_factors: P/E diluted=9.633x at bottom of 1-year range (9.5..60.7x), signaling deep value vs own history
ROE=0.189 and net_margin=0.135 indicate strong profitability even at recent oil levels
Q2 2022 EPS=5.95 more than doubled vs Q4 2021 EPS=2.63, showing powerful earnings trajectory
Price pulled back ~12% from $164.63 (8/29) to $143.67 (9/30), creating a better entry despite 1y return still +37.7%
News flags oil-price risk ('$80 Oil Price Won't Last', 9/28) and sector plunge (9/29), capping upside conviction

sources_cited.excerpt: Chevron And XLE: $80 Oil Price Won't Last
Why Oil and Gas Stocks Plummeted Today
3 of the Cheapest, Safest Stocks You Can Buy Today (mentions CVX)

WMT.rationale: WMT trades at a deeply discounted 8.5x P/E with 16.4% ROE and stable margins, offering attractive risk-adjusted reward despite near-term consumer pressure and inventory concerns.
key_factors: pe_diluted=8.468x vs 1y range 8.1..16.4 — near 1-year low
ROE=16.4% with operating_margin=4.1% and gross_margin=24.6% — durable profitability
Q2 FY23 revenue=152.86B, net_income=5.15B, eps=1.88 — strong sequential recovery vs Q1 eps=0.74
ret_1y=-5.4% with ret_3m=+4.4% — price stabilizing after drawdown
News flags inventory glut risk ('1 Shocking Number') and holiday hiring trims — near-term headwinds

sources_cited.excerpt: 1 Shocking Number That Could Spell Trouble for Walmart Stock
Retailers are trimming holiday hiring plans

DIS.rationale: DIS has been crushed (-46.4% 1y) and valuation has compressed to the low end of its recent PE range, with quarterly EPS inflecting higher and an activist catalyst in play, but depressed margins and DTC-driven estimate cuts keep the risk-adjusted reward only mildly attractive.
key_factors: PE_diluted=49.1x sits at the LOW end of 1y range (49.1..200.4), signaling valuation reset after -46.4% 1y drawdown
Quarterly EPS trajectory improving: $0.26 (Q2) → $0.60 (Q1) → $0.77 (Q3 fiscal 2022), with revenue rebounding to $21.50B
Activist Third Point secured a new board seat (2022-09-30), a potential catalyst for value-unlocking change
Analyst profit estimates being cut on DTC losses and content licensing guidance (2022-09-30) — offsets the bullish setup
Net margin only 4.3% and ROE 3.6% — earnings power still depressed, so the 49x PE is not cheap on absolute basis

sources_cited.excerpt: Disney names new board member in deal with activist investor Third Point
Disney Analyst Slashes Profit Estimates To Factor In Content Sales Licensing Guidance, DTC Losses
